assumptions: No Rootly account, organization membership, Slack integration, connected Slack user, team configuration, or incident is assumed.
A Slack Free, Pro, or Business+ workspace and Slack Workspace Admin or Owner access are available for the otherwise documented downstream route.
A work email and password would be used for the documented signup route; no SSO-enforced domain or prior organization invitation is assumed.
No account, agreement, Slack authorization, integration, channel, or incident was created during research.

developer_goal: Create a Rootly account, connect a standard Slack workspace, create a first test incident, and observe its dedicated incident channel and initial system message.

first_success.outcome: A test incident is created in Rootly's web app and the browser redirects to its incident page.
first_success.observable_signal: The newly created incident page opens with the incident record, lifecycle timestamp, and initial timeline entry.

route_selection.surface: Rootly quick start from signup through a first Slack test incident
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Rootly quick start from signup through a first Slack test incident
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Dashboard Create Test Incident
candidates.reason: The quick start calls this the fastest dashboard route, but its detailed setup spine still includes connecting Slack and does not document the dashboard modal field sequence on that page.

candidates.name: Microsoft Teams integration
candidates.reason: The quick-start navigation presents Microsoft Teams as a peer communication surface with different installation, permission, and channel actions.

candidates.name: Google Chat integration
candidates.reason: The quick-start navigation presents Google Chat as another peer communication surface rather than selecting Slack for every organization.

required_path.action: Complete Rootly account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Rootly account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Rootly candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The newly created incident page opens with the incident record, lifecycle timestamp, and initial timeline entry.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'identity', 'requirement': 'Choose Google, Slack, SSO, email and password, or an organization invitation for Rootly identity; the candidate uses email and password.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S3', 'S4']}
{'order': 2, 'type': 'legal', 'requirement': "The email-and-password route requires acceptance of Rootly's Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.", 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S3', 'S4']}

{'order': 3, 'type': 'permission', 'requirement': 'The standard Slack installation requires a Rootly account with Admin or Owner permissions and a Slack Workspace Admin or Owner account.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S5']}

external_gates.description: Google, Slack, SSO, email and password, and invitation routes change identity and organization-entry actions.
The signup guide does not establish that a fresh self-service account has the Rootly Admin permission required by the Slack installation guide.
Slack setup requires Rootly Admin or Owner and Slack Workspace Admin or Owner permissions; Enterprise Grid requires an Org Owner or Admin.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.
Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Rootly show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
